text.title: Django Model Design Checklist
text.summary: Ordered checklist covering planning, model definition, relationships, constraints, indexing, migrations, and documentation phases.

list.item: Identify all entities and relationships
Determine field types and constraints for each entity
Plan ForeignKey on_delete behavior for each relationship
Identify fields that need indexes (FK fields, frequently filtered fields)
Plan timestamped fields (use BaseModel)
Create BaseModel abstract class in core/models.py if not present
Define all models inheriting from BaseModel
Add field validators and help_text for non-obvious fields
Define __str__ methods
Create custom QuerySets for common filters
Add Meta options: ordering, indexes, db_table names
Put all TextChoices in constants.py
Define ForeignKey with explicit on_delete
Add related_name= on every FK (use '+' if reverse not needed)
Use lazy string references ('app.Model') to avoid circular imports
Replace unique_together with UniqueConstraint(fields=[], name="...")
Verify FK fields have database indexes (Django adds them automatically)
Add composite indexes to Meta.indexes for common filter combinations
Name every index: appname_model_cols_idx
Remove redundant db_index=True when uniqueness already implies an index
Run makemigrations --check to detect unstaged model changes
Review migration operations for destructive changes (RemoveField, AlterField on PKs)
Test migration on a fresh database before committing
Never edit applied migration files; create a new migration instead

text.reference: Pre-commit script refusing commits that changed models without a staged migration.
